task_completion.description: Create a single @ParameterizedTest method with test data.

task_completion.description: Remove the 8 redundant individual test methods.

The class should primarily contain the new parameterized test.

task_completion.description: Run the tests and verify all 8 scenarios pass.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A test execution panel in the IDE or terminal output (e.g., Maven Surefire) showing that the tests passed successfully, specifically indicating that 8 test cases (or 8 invocations of the parameterized test) were executed and passed.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI-based editor or IDE to refactor the code.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a graphical editor like IntelliJ IDEA, VS Code, or Eclipse being used to edit the Java file, rather than just terminal commands echoing text.

integrity.description: The test results come from an actual test runner.
integrity.visual_evidence: The test success is shown in a genuine IDE test runner panel (with green checkmarks and execution trees) or standard Maven terminal output ('BUILD SUCCESS', 'Tests run: 8'), not a manually typed text file.

integrity.description: The parameterized test actually tests the PasswordValidator.
integrity.visual_evidence: The body of the new parameterized test method contains a call to the actual validation logic (e.g., 'PasswordValidator.isValid(...)') and asserts against its result, rather than containing a hardcoded 'assertTrue(true)' bypass.
